    CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.forget
    CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver

    CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver_map

    CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.instFullCostructuredArrowTopOverToOver
    CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.HasFactorization.over

    Subcategories of comma categories defined by morphism properties #

    Given functors L : A ⥤ T and R : B ⥤ T and morphism properties P, Q and W on T, A and B respectively, we define the subcategory P.Comma L R Q W of Comma L R where

    • objects are objects of Comma L R with the structural morphism satisfying P, and
    • morphisms are morphisms of Comma L R where the left morphism satisfies Q and the right morphism satisfies W.

    For an object X : T, this specializes to P.Over Q X which is the subcategory of Over X where the structural morphism satisfies P and where the horizontal morphisms satisfy Q. Common examples of the latter are e.g. the category of schemes étale (finite, affine, etc.) over a base X. Here Q = ⊤.

    Implementation details #

    • We provide the general constructor P.Comma L R Q W to obtain Over X and Under X as special cases of the more general setup.

    • Most results are developed only in the case where Q = ⊤ and W = ⊤, but the definition is setup in the general case to allow for a later generalization if needed.

    source
    theorem CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.costructuredArrow_iso_iff {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (P : MorphismProperty T) [P.RespectsIso] {L : Functor A T} {X : T} {f g : CostructuredArrow L X} (e : f ≅ g) :
    P f.hom ↔ P g.hom
    source
    theorem CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.structuredArrow_iso_iff {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (P : MorphismProperty T) [P.RespectsIso] {L : Functor A T} {X : T} {f g : StructuredArrow X L} (e : f ≅ g) :
    P f.hom ↔ P g.hom
    source
    theorem CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.over_iso_iff {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (P : MorphismProperty T) [P.RespectsIso] {X : T} {f g : Over X} (e : f ≅ g) :
    P f.hom ↔ P g.hom
    source
    theorem CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.under_iso_iff {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (P : MorphismProperty T) [P.RespectsIso] {X : T} {f g : Under X} (e : f ≅ g) :
    P f.hom ↔ P g.hom
    source
    def CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.commaObj {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {B : Type u_2} [Category.{v_2, u_2} B] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (L : Functor A T) (R : Functor B T) (W : MorphismProperty T) :
    ObjectProperty (Comma L R)

    The object property on Comma L R induced by a morphism property.

                  structure CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {B : Type u_2} [Category.{v_2, u_2} B] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (L : Functor A T) (R : Functor B T) (P : MorphismProperty T) (Q : MorphismProperty A) (W : MorphismProperty B) extends CategoryTheory.Comma L R :
                  Type (max (max u_1 u_2) v_3)

                  P.Comma L R Q W is the subcategory of Comma L R consisting of objects X : Comma L R where X.hom satisfies P. The morphisms are given by morphisms in Comma L R where the left one satisfies Q and the right one satisfies W.

                  • left : A
                  • right : B
                  • hom : L.obj self.left ⟶ R.obj self.right
                  • prop : P self.hom
                  Instances For

                    theorem CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.ext {A : Type u_1} {inst✝ : Category.{v_1, u_1} A} {B : Type u_2} {inst✝¹ : Category.{v_2, u_2} B} {T : Type u_3} {inst✝² : Category.{v_3, u_3} T} {L : Functor A T} {R : Functor B T} {P : MorphismProperty T} {Q : MorphismProperty A} {W : MorphismProperty B} {x y : MorphismProperty.Comma L R P Q W} (left : x.left = y.left) (right : x.right = y.right) (hom : x.hom ≍ y.hom) :
                    x = y
                    source
                    theorem CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.ext_iff {A : Type u_1} {inst✝ : Category.{v_1, u_1} A} {B : Type u_2} {inst✝¹ : Category.{v_2, u_2} B} {T : Type u_3} {inst✝² : Category.{v_3, u_3} T} {L : Functor A T} {R : Functor B T} {P : MorphismProperty T} {Q : MorphismProperty A} {W : MorphismProperty B} {x y : MorphismProperty.Comma L R P Q W} :
                    x = y ↔ x.left = y.left ∧ x.right = y.right ∧ x.hom ≍ y.hom
                    source
                    structure CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.Hom {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {B : Type u_2} [Category.{v_2, u_2} B] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] {L : Functor A T} {R : Functor B T} {P : MorphismProperty T} {Q : MorphismProperty A} {W : MorphismProperty B} (X Y : MorphismProperty.Comma L R P Q W) extends CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism X.toComma Y.toComma :
                    Type (max v_1 v_2)

                    A morphism in P.Comma L R Q W is a morphism in Comma L R where the left hom satisfies Q and the right one satisfies W.

                    • left : X.left ⟶ Y.left
                    • right : X.right ⟶ Y.right
                    • w : CategoryStruct.comp (L.map self.left) Y.hom = CategoryStruct.comp X.hom (R.map self.right)
                    • prop_hom_left : Q self.left
                    • prop_hom_right : W self.right

                      x = y ↔ x.left = y.left ∧ x.right = y.right

                                                def CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.mapLeftId {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {B : Type u_2} [Category.{v_2, u_2} B] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (L : Functor A T) (R : Functor B T) {P : MorphismProperty T} {Q : MorphismProperty A} {W : MorphismProperty B} [Q.IsMultiplicative] [W.IsMultiplicative] [Q.RespectsIso] [W.RespectsIso] :
                                                mapLeft R (CategoryStruct.id L) ⋯ ≅ Functor.id (MorphismProperty.Comma L R P Q W)

                                                The functor P.Comma L R Q W ⥤ P.Comma L R Q W induced by the identity natural transformation on L is naturally isomorphic to the identity functor.

                                                  def CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.mapLeftComp {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {B : Type u_2} [Category.{v_2, u_2} B] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (R : Functor B T) {P : MorphismProperty T} {Q : MorphismProperty A} {W : MorphismProperty B} [Q.IsMultiplicative] [W.IsMultiplicative] {L₁ L₂ L₃ : Functor A T} [Q.RespectsIso] [W.RespectsIso] (l : L₁ ⟶ L₂) (l' : L₂ ⟶ L₃) (hl : ∀ (X : MorphismProperty.Comma L₂ R P Q W), P (CategoryStruct.comp (l.app X.left) X.hom)) (hl' : ∀ (X : MorphismProperty.Comma L₃ R P Q W), P (CategoryStruct.comp (l'.app X.left) X.hom)) (hll' : ∀ (X : MorphismProperty.Comma L₃ R P Q W), P (CategoryStruct.comp ((CategoryStruct.comp l l').app X.left) X.hom)) :
                                                  mapLeft R (CategoryStruct.comp l l') hll' ≅ (mapLeft R l' hl').comp (mapLeft R l hl)

                                                  The functor P.Comma L₁ R Q W ⥤ P.Comma L₃ R Q W induced by the composition of two natural transformations l : L₁ ⟶ L₂ and l' : L₂ ⟶ L₃ is naturally isomorphic to the composition of the two functors induced by these natural transformations.

                                                    mapLeft R l hl ≅ mapLeft R l' ⋯

                                                    Two equal natural transformations L₁ ⟶ L₂ yield naturally isomorphic functors P.Comma L₁ R Q W ⥤ P.Comma L₂ R Q W.

                                                      MorphismProperty.Comma L₁ R P Q W ≌ MorphismProperty.Comma L₂ R P Q W

                                                      A natural isomorphism L₁ ≅ L₂ induces an equivalence of categories P.Comma L₁ R Q W ≌ P.Comma L₂ R Q W.

                                                          def CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.mapRightId {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {B : Type u_2} [Category.{v_2, u_2} B] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (L : Functor A T) (R : Functor B T) {P : MorphismProperty T} {Q : MorphismProperty A} {W : MorphismProperty B} [Q.IsMultiplicative] [W.IsMultiplicative] [Q.RespectsIso] [W.RespectsIso] :
                                                          mapRight L (CategoryStruct.id R) ⋯ ≅ Functor.id (MorphismProperty.Comma L R P Q W)

                                                          The functor P.Comma L R Q W ⥤ P.Comma L R Q W induced by the identity natural transformation on R is naturally isomorphic to the identity functor.

                                                            def CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.mapRightComp {A : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} A] {B : Type u_2} [Category.{v_2, u_2} B] {T : Type u_3} [Category.{v_3, u_3} T] (L : Functor A T) {P : MorphismProperty T} {Q : MorphismProperty A} {W : MorphismProperty B} [Q.IsMultiplicative] [W.IsMultiplicative] {R₁ R₂ R₃ : Functor B T} [Q.RespectsIso] [W.RespectsIso] (r : R₁ ⟶ R₂) (r' : R₂ ⟶ R₃) (hr : ∀ (X : MorphismProperty.Comma L R₁ P Q W), P (CategoryStruct.comp X.hom (r.app X.right))) (hr' : ∀ (X : MorphismProperty.Comma L R₂ P Q W), P (CategoryStruct.comp X.hom (r'.app X.right))) (hrr' : ∀ (X : MorphismProperty.Comma L R₁ P Q W), P (CategoryStruct.comp X.hom ((CategoryStruct.comp r r').app X.right))) :
                                                            mapRight L (CategoryStruct.comp r r') hrr' ≅ (mapRight L r hr).comp (mapRight L r' hr')

                                                            The functor P.Comma L R₁ Q W ⥤ P.Comma L R₃ Q W induced by the composition of the natural transformations r : R₁ ⟶ R₂ and r' : R₂ ⟶ R₃ is naturally isomorphic to the composition of the functors induced by these natural transformations.

                                                                  @[reducible, inline]
                                                                  abbrev CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Arrow {T : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} T] (P Q W : MorphismProperty T) :
                                                                  Type (max v_1 u_1)

                                                                  Given a morphism property P on a category T, this is the subcategory of Arrow T defined by P where morphisms satisfy Q and W on the left and right, respectively.

                                                                  Equations
                                                                  • P.Arrow Q W = CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma (CategoryTheory.Functor.id T) (CategoryTheory.Functor.id T) P Q W
                                                                  Instances For
                                                                    source
                                                                    @[reducible, inline]
                                                                    abbrev CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Arrow.forget {T : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} T] (P Q W : MorphismProperty T) [Q.IsMultiplicative] [W.IsMultiplicative] :
                                                                    Functor (P.Arrow Q W) (Arrow T)

                                                                    The forgetful functor from the full subcategory of Arrow T defined by P to Arrow T.

                                                                    Equations
                                                                    • CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Arrow.forget P Q W = CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Comma.forget (CategoryTheory.Functor.id T) (CategoryTheory.Functor.id T) P Q W

                                                                      (Arrow.forget P ⊤ ⊤).Full

                                                                      ((Arrow.forget P Q W).comp Arrow.leftFunc).map f = f.left

                                                                      Occasionally useful for rewriting in the backwards direction.

                                                                      ((Arrow.forget P Q W).comp Arrow.rightFunc).map f = f.right

                                                                      Occasionally useful for rewriting in the backwards direction.

                                                                      def CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Arrow.Hom.mk {T : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} T] {P Q W : MorphismProperty T} [Q.IsMultiplicative] [W.IsMultiplicative] {A B : P.Arrow Q W} (f : (Arrow.forget P Q W).obj A ⟶ (Arrow.forget P Q W).obj B) (hfl : Q (Arrow.Hom.left f)) (hfr : W (Arrow.Hom.right f)) :
                                                                      A ⟶ B

                                                                      Construct a morphism in P.Arrow Q W from a morphism in Arrow T.

                                                                      Equations
                                                                      • CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Arrow.Hom.mk f hfl hfr = { toCommaMorphism := f, prop_hom_left := hfl, prop_hom_right := hfr }

                                                                        P.Arrow Q W

                                                                        Make an object of P.Arrow Q X from a morphism f : A ⟶ B and a proof of P f.

                                                                        Equations
                                                                        • CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Arrow.mk f hf = { left := A, right := B, hom := f, prop := hf }

                                                                                abbrev CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Over {T : Type u_1} [Category.{v_1, u_1} T] (P Q : MorphismProperty T) (X : T) :
                                                                                Type (max v_1 u_1)

                                                                                Given a morphism property P on a category T and an object X : T, this is the subcategory of Over X defined by P where morphisms satisfy Q.

                                                                                Equations

                                                                                  Functor (P.Over Q X) (Over X)

                                                                                                                A ≅ B

                                                                                                                Construct an morphism in P.CostructuredArrow Q F X by giving the isomorphism on the underlying objects of C.

                                                                                                                    Functor (P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ F X) (P.Over ⊤ X)

                                                                                                                      theorem CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver_map {C : Type u_1} {D : Type u_2} [Category.{u_3, u_1} C] [Category.{u_4, u_2} D] (P : MorphismProperty D) (F : Functor C D) (X : D) {X✝ Y✝ : P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ F X} (f : X✝ ⟶ Y✝) :
                                                                                                                      (CostructuredArrow.toOver P F X).map f = Over.homMk (F.map f.left) ⋯ ⋯
